title: Hattie the Mouse and the Poet Worm
summary: On a rainy morning on Mouse Street, Hattie the Mouse enjoys toast and hibiscus tea while a thunderstorm keeps her indoors. To pass the time, she draws a mysterious picture with a black marker. Hearing a strange sound outside, she discovers a worm squelching through the mud. The worm is working on a poem about muck and asks Hattie for help with rhymes. After brainstorming words like 'yuck' and 'buck' and finishing their special project, the two friends head home for a peaceful afternoon nap.

highlightQuote.text: Hibiscus. And she lived on Mouse Street.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Once upon a time, Hattie the mouse was sitting at home. It was a grey rainy morning. The rain was dripping down all through the branches and leaves of her tree. Dripple-dripple-dap-dap-dap, splashing in little puddles all around her house.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hey, hey Hattie, your plants love the rain.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Hattie thought to herself, my plants do love the rain. That's a nice thing about the rain. And what else did she think to herself?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know. You keep telling story and then you'll find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that's true. I will find out what happens if I just keep telling the story. And so, Hattie the mouse had her breakfast. What did she have?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Toast and peanut butter.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: She had toast and peanut butter. And what did she have to drink?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She had juice. No, she had tea.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: She had some delicious tea. Oh, what flavor?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hibiscus. And she lived on Mouse Street.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, Mouse Street, yes. And as Hattie the mouse was finishing her breakfast, she noticed that the rain was slowing down. Dipple-dipple-dap-da-da. And the grey clouds were starting to move away. And as she looked out her window, she could see just a little tiny speck of blue sky. And then all of a sudden, ta-da! It was bright because the sun came out. And so what did she do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She wanted to play outside but there was a thunderstorm.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: There was a thunderstorm, so she decided to stay inside for a little while longer. What did she do inside?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She just made a picture.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, a picture. What was her picture of?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A little picture of a thunderstorm.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A picture of a thunderstorm. And what did she use to draw it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Black marker.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Some black marker. And did she use any other colors?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Uh-uh.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Just the black marker. She made a big picture of the thunderstorm with black marker. And did it have clouds?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: No. What did it have in the picture?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It had nothing. You keep telling story and then you'll find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. Well, the funny sound that she heard, she didn't know what it was. And so she opened her window and poked her head out and she said, oh my gosh! I didn't know who was making that sound, but now I know because I see you out there. And who did she see?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Nobody.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I think she saw somebody.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Nobody!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It wasn't a person, it was a bug. What kind of bug was it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was a worm.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It was a worm, going wiggle-wiggle-wobble-wobble and squishing and squelching its way through the mud. And the worm said, 'Um, hi Hattie.' And Hattie said, 'Oh, hello worm, how are you doing today on this fine slightly rainy day?' And the worm said, 'I'm, um, pretty good, but um, I need your help with a project.' And Hattie said, 'Oh, what's the project?' 'I'm writing a, um, poem.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know. You must keep telling the story and then you'll find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And the worm said, 'My poem is about, um, muck. My favorite topic. The muck. Because that's what worms like. But I need a rhyme. Will you help me with that? What rhymes with, um, muck?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yuck.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, yuck! Why didn't I think of that? Well, the poem will begin: I love the muck, I don't say yuck. Hmm, I need more to my poem, Hattie. What else could I add?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Buck.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Buck? Oh. I have a buck. What else?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The story ends with one more thing they're going to do. A process, that means project. And they did the project and then they decided to go home. And then they went home and then they napped. The end.

characters.name: Hattie the Mouse
characters.description: A nimble, friendly mouse of Possible Street and loyal friend to Izzy and Seeker, this brave artisan is the wife of Dimitar and mistress of the Workshop of Hats—a weathered hollow tree sanctuary on Cornell Street scented with cedar, ink, and peanut butter toast. Clad in her signature red cloak and pointy hat, she is a resourceful hatmaker whose spirit was forged against massive diggers and the rain-slicked Floofer Cloud. Within her paint-stained home, she carefully curates her husband’s library alongside her own hand-painted volumes while confronting a baffling supply mystery; the discovery of curious clover stickers signals a gathering shadow of intrigue she is determined to unravel.

characters.name: The Worm
characters.description: A bug that lives in the mud and writes poetry about muck.

places.name: Mouse Street
places.description: A long, storied thoroughfare guiding explorers into the heart of Mouseland, this bustling street serves as both the cherished home of Hattie the Mouse and the vital hub for the community’s doctor’s office.

places.name: Hattie's House
places.description: Nestled in a sun-drenched tree across from the town park, Hattie’s arboreal sanctuary on Mouse Street doubles as a vibrant Cornell Street workshop for designing hats and a peaceful retreat for resting after long days of painting. This cozy home, cluttered with the tools of her trade, is a place of shared morning breakfasts with Mischief and savory meals cooked alongside her Nana. Whether lulled by the rhythmic drumming of rainfall or the quiet exhaustion of her artistry, Hattie finds her solace here, drifting into restorative naps amidst the creative clutter.
